name: The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa
name_original: La Muela de Millares y el Rincón del Camaro — Circular desde Quesa
region_intro: Mountain biking around Quesa offers diverse landscapes within the Sangre de Cristo Mountains and access to the Carson National Forest. The region features remote off-road routes that traverse forested trails and varied terrain, including rocky sections. Its unique soil composition allows for quick drainage, often keeping trails open even after heavy rain. This area provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Quesa The most popular mountain bike trail is El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp, a 31.6 miles (50.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa, a difficult 38.4 miles (61.8 km) path. This route offers extensive views and challenging climbs, characteristic of the region's mountainous landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Prince's Spring – Mortirolo of Quesa loop from Navarrés, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail leading through forested areas and hills, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Mountain biking around Quesa is defined by forested trails, diverse terrain with rocky sections, and routes through the Sangre de Cristo Mountains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Quesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 19 reviews. More than 277 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Quesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Quesa?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Quesa, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy routes to challenging, difficult paths.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Quesa?
A: The trails around Quesa feature diverse terrain, including forested paths and rocky sections. Many routes traverse the Sangre de Cristo Mountains, offering varied landscapes. The unique soil composition in some areas allows for quick drainage, meaning trails often remain rideable even after heavy rain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Quesa?
A: Yes, Quesa offers several easy mountain bike trails, with 6 routes specifically categorized as easy. These are great for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Quesa is known for its challenging routes, with 29 trails categorized as difficult. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909564069/el-caroche-circular-desde-bicorp-por-la-solana-de-la-muela-de-bicorp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 31.6-mile trail with significant elevation gain, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908615157/the-muela-de-millares-and-the-rincon-del-camaro-circular-from-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult 38.4-mile path offering extensive views and challenging climbs.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Quesa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Quesa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 19 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the extensive views found along routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908615157/the-muela-de-millares-and-the-rincon-del-camaro-circular-from-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Quesa?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Quesa are circular. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909564069/el-caroche-circular-desde-bicorp-por-la-solana-de-la-muela-de-bicorp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prince's Spring – Mortirolo of Quesa loop from Navarrés&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through forested areas and hills.

Q: What natural features or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the stunning mountainous landscapes, you can explore various natural attractions. Nearby highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4069862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Araña Cave and La Rebolla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, other caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4340471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Tornero&lt;/a&gt;, and beautiful lakes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool on the Sellent River in Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3420653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escalona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Quesa?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the region offers a range of difficulties, including 6 easy trails. These easier options are generally more suitable for families with children or those looking for a less strenuous ride. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Quesa?
A: Quesa's unique soil composition allows for quick drainage, meaning trails are often open even after heavy rain. This suggests that riding is possible for much of the year. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn typically offer milder temperatures, while summer can be quite warm. Winter might offer different conditions, but the region is also known for winter recreation.

Q: Are there any services for mountain bikers in Quesa, such as cafes or accommodation?
A: While specific cafes or bike services are not detailed in the trail information, Quesa is a destination for various outdoor activities, suggesting that local amenities for visitors, including accommodation and places to eat, are available. For more information on local services, you might find resources on the &lt;a href="https://visitquesta.com/adventures/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Questa website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Quesa?
A: Specific parking information for individual trailheads is not provided in the guide. However, as a popular outdoor destination, it's reasonable to expect parking facilities near common access points for trails. It's advisable to check local maps or specific route descriptions on komoot for details on parking availability at your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer great views?
A: Yes, many trails in Quesa offer spectacular views, especially those that traverse the mountainous landscape. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908615157/the-muela-de-millares-and-the-rincon-del-camaro-circular-from-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa&lt;/a&gt; is known for its extensive views, characteristic of the region's mountainous terrain.
tips: Very picturesque site worth visiting. From Millares you can get there by cycling, from Rambla Tambuc the descent is very technical and I recommend doing it on foot, it's a few meters and the views are incredible all the way.
